cards.title: Balancing Work and Childcare This Summer
cards.body: As school holidays begin, many parents face the annual challenge of juggling work with childcare responsibilities. The rising cost of holiday care makes this balancing act even more difficult for families.

cards.title: Surrogacy in the UK: Legal Challenges
cards.body: UK surrogacy law is struggling to keep pace with rising demand, forcing intended parents to seek riskier arrangements abroad. Experts are urging ministers to update legislation to better protect all parties involved.

cards.title: Creating Meaningful Rituals for Your Child
cards.body: A parent describes creating a special high tea ritual to mark her daughter's transition into her teens. These intentional moments help children feel valued and create lasting memories.

cards.title: Picky Eater Labels May Worsen Behavior
cards.body: Calling your child a "picky eater" could actually make picky eating worse by reinforcing the behavior. Small language shifts can help kids become more confident and adventurous with food.

cards.title: Raising Teens for Healthy Relationships
cards.body: The best way to protect your teen isn't by preventing dating but by teaching them to recognize respect and set boundaries. Equipping teens with these skills helps them choose healthier relationships.

cards.title: When Should Neurodivergent Kids Get Phones?
cards.body: Instead of relying on age, focus on your neurodivergent child's developmental readiness when considering their first cell phone. This approach helps ensure they can manage the responsibility appropriately.

cards.title: Talking to Kids About Your Mortality
cards.body: Parents are increasingly having conversations with their children about death and mortality, as modeled by public figures like Angelina Jolie. These difficult discussions help children understand and process life's realities.

cards.title: Cell Phone Age for Neurodivergent Children
cards.body: Developmental readiness matters more than chronological age when deciding if your neurodivergent child is ready for a cell phone. Parents should assess their child's ability to use technology responsibly.

cards.title: Sensitive Teens and Social Media Challenges
cards.body: Sensitive teens face unique pressures from social media and peer comparison, which can impact their self-worth. With time and support, their social world expands to include more accepting communities.

cards.title: Death and Childhood: Important Conversations
cards.body: Teaching children about mortality at a young age helps them develop resilience and understanding of life's natural cycles. These conversations prevent postponing difficult emotional work until adulthood.
